name: kind-integration
description: Kind cluster integration: kind bootstrap script delivers a running sovereign-test cluster with Cilium CNI, cert-manager, sealed-secrets, and autarky egress policy verified.
sprintGoal: kind bootstrap (cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh) runs end-to-end, produces a valid cluster-values.yaml, and the kind cluster passes all autarky invariants.

stories.title: ANDON: Restore broken GGE — Planning pipeline always has a pending increment
stories.description: GGE G5 indicator is failing. Add a pending increment to manifest.json.
acceptanceCriteria: GGE G5 indicator passes: python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')"
stories.testPlan: Run: python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')" — must exit 0 and print OK. Show output verbatim.

stories.title: Plan ceremony: always queue a pending increment stub when creating an active sprint
stories.description: GGE G5 fires every sprint because the plan ceremony creates an active increment but never queues a follow-on pending increment. The fix: at the end of the plan ceremony, if no increment has status='pending' in manifest.json, automatically append a minimal pending increment stub (id, name, description, status='pending', dependsOn current). This ensures GGE G5 never fires again and the planning pipeline is always primed.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md (or the plan ceremony runner) contains logic to append a pending increment stub if none exists

After running plan ceremony on a fresh sprint, python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')" exits 0
shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies/*.sh exits 0 (if any .sh files are modified)
stories.returnedReason: Root cause of recurring GGE-G5 ANDON: plan ceremony does not queue a pending increment after creating an active sprint, so G5 fires every sprint cycle.
stories.testPlan: After implementing the plan ceremony change: run the plan ceremony on a fresh sprint context, then verify: python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')" — must exit 0 and print OK. Show output verbatim. Also: shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies/*.sh must exit 0.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: AC targets 'plan.md (or the plan ceremony runner)' — both candidate files should be named explicitly so Ralph knows exactly which file to edit. Measurable score 4: AC1 ('contains logic to append a pending increment stub') is a prose assertion, not a runnable command; replace with a grep for the specific function or code pattern added.

stories.title: Kaizen: Pre-retro guard — auto-run review before retro if passes:true stories exist
stories.description: ceremonies.sh currently fires the retro ceremony immediately when invoked, even if there are stories with passes:true, reviewed:false. This causes advance.py to error with 'limbo stories' and requires manual retro intervention. Fix: add a pre-retro check in ceremonies.sh that runs the review ceremony first if any sprint story has passes:true and reviewed:false.
acceptanceCriteria: ceremonies.sh (or the retro ceremony wrapper) checks for passes:true reviewed:false stories before running retro

If such stories exist, review ceremony is invoked first: python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('prd/increment-17-restructure.json')); pending=[x for x in s['stories'] if x.get('passes') and not x.get('reviewed')]; print(len(pending))" returns 0 after the guard runs
shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh exits 0 after the change
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py exits 0 (if Python-based)
stories.testPlan: Manually set a story to passes:true reviewed:false in a test sprint file. Run ceremonies.sh --start-at retro. Verify review ceremony runs first, then retro. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: AC2 hard-codes 'increment-17-restructure.json' as the test target — a completed sprint. The guard must work for the active sprint file; AC should use a temporary fixture file, not a named completed increment. Measurable score 4: AC1 ('checks for passes:true reviewed:false stories') is prose; should be grep/python command that verifies the guard code exists in ceremonies.sh.

stories.title: kind cluster bootstrap produces a valid cluster-values.yaml
stories.description: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh runs end-to-end on a developer machine. Produces cluster-values.yaml that passes contract validation. Kind cluster has 3 nodes, Cilium CNI (kindnet disabled), cert-manager, sealed-secrets, local-path-provisioner, MinIO.
acceptanceCriteria: ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run exits 0
./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh runs without error on a clean machine
kind get clusters shows sovereign-test
kubectl get nodes shows 3 nodes all Ready
python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml exits 0
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n minio shows all Running
kubectl get storageclass shows local-path as default
stories.testPlan: 1. ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run: must exit 0. Show output. 2. ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh: runs without error. 3. kind get clusters: shows sovereign-test. 4. kubectl get nodes: shows 3 nodes all Ready. 5. python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml: exits 0. 6. kubectl get pods -n cert-manager: all Running. 7. kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets: all Running. 8. kubectl get pods -n minio: all Running. 9. kubectl get storageclass: shows local-path as default. Show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Achievable score 3: 3-point story requires authoring cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh from scratch PLUS installing Cilium (with kindnet disabled), cert-manager, sealed-secrets, MinIO, local-path-provisioner, and a contract validator — eight integration points that together exceed a single Ralph iteration budget. Recommend splitting: KIND-001a (bootstrap.sh + 3-node cluster + contract/validate.py) and KIND-001b (Cilium + cert-manager + sealed-secrets + MinIO). Time-bound score 4: MinIO and local-path-provisioner appear in ACs but not in the sprint goal or story description — minor scope creep risk that could expand during implementation.

stories.title: Kaizen: Rename retro-patch-phase*.md → retro-patch-increment*.md
stories.description: The retro ceremony generates patch files named retro-patch-phase<N>.md. The word 'phase' is retired from the data model — all concepts use 'increment'. Rename the generation pattern in retro.md and sync.md ceremonies, and rename any existing retro-patch-phase*.md files to retro-patch-increment*.md.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md no longer contains the string 'retro-patch-phase'
scripts/ralph/ceremonies/sync.md no longer contains the string 'retro-patch-phase'
grep -r 'retro-patch-phase' scripts/ returns no matches
shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh exits 0 after any shell changes
stories.testPlan: grep -r 'retro-patch-phase' scripts/ — must return no matches. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Relevant score 4: naming consistency kaizen is valuable but not on the critical path to the sprint goal (kind cluster bootstrap). Can be deferred without blocking KIND-001.

stories.title: Kaizen: Remove legacy 'phase' field from backlog stories
stories.description: Eight backlog stories have a redundant 'phase' integer field that duplicates information already encoded in 'epicId → targetIncrement'. This field drifts from reality as stories move between increments. Remove the 'phase' field from all backlog stories and update the story schema to mark it as deprecated.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); has_phase=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'phase' in s]; assert len(has_phase)==0, f'stories still have phase field: {has_phase}'; print('OK')" exits 0 and prints OK
grep -n '"phase"' prd/backlog.json returns no matches
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('prd/backlog.json'))" exits 0 (valid JSON)
stories.testPlan: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); has_phase=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'phase' in s]; assert len(has_phase)==0; print('OK')" — must exit 0 and print OK. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Relevant score 4: data model cleanup is useful but not blocking the sprint goal. Can be deferred without impact to KIND-001.
